THE COURT WILL NOT DETERMINE THE ADEQUACY OF THOSE RESPONSES ON THIS MOTION. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE LATE RESPONSES WERE A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AGAINST DEFENDANT IN THE AMOUNT OF $690, TO BE PAID WITHIN 20 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. ORDER WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. KAREN Y. UCHIYAMA, ESQ., (415)563-9301, FOR PLAINTIFF/OPPOSING PARTY, JOHN BRITTON

THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT KAZMER UJVAROSY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT RAISED BY THE DECLARATIONS OF PLAINTIFF AND HIS ATTORNEY WHETHER PLAINTIFF'S CASHING OF THE JULY RENT CHECK WAS INADVERTENT VERSUS BEING AN INTENTIONAL WAIVER. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Have Genuinessness Of Documents And Truth Of Admissions Deemed Admitted, For Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions; Declaration Of Karen Y. Uchiyama In Support Thereof; And P/A In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Jul-31-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: Karen Uchiyama, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: John Britton; James Driscoll, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: Kazmer Ujvarosy

HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Plaintiff John Britton's Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Have Genuineness Of Documents And Truth Of Admissions Deemed Admitted; For Production Of Documents is moot as responses have been served. The court will not determine the adequacy of those responses on this motion. The court finds that the late responses were a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant in the amount of $690, to be paid within 20 days of notice of this order. ORDER WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED
